name: Netdata API
what_it_does: Real-time infrastructure monitoring agent with REST API providing 1-second resolution metrics for Linux systems, containers, and applications without sampling or averaging.
use_cases: Querying per-second CPU, memory, disk, and network metrics for performance analysis agents
Alerting on infrastructure anomalies detected by Netdata's built-in ML engine
Correlating system metrics across nodes for distributed system debugging
Building real-time dashboards that embed Netdata charts via API
Detecting resource contention and saturation for capacity planning automation
not_for: Long-term metric storage without Netdata Cloud or external integration
Application-level tracing (Netdata focuses on system and infrastructure metrics)
Organizations needing ITSM compliance or enterprise audit trails
best_when: An agent needs real-time, high-resolution system metrics from Linux hosts without metric sampling or aggregation delay.
avoid_when: You need distributed tracing, log aggregation, or multi-year metric retention from a single platform.

auth.notes: Netdata Cloud uses JWT bearer tokens for API access. Self-hosted local API requires no authentication by default (localhost) or IP whitelisting. Netdata Cloud adds centralized auth.

pricing.free_tier_limits: Netdata Agent: open-source, free. Netdata Cloud Free: unlimited nodes, 14-day retention
pricing.notes: Agent is AGPL-3.0 open-source and fully functional standalone. Cloud adds central coordination, longer retention, and team features.

performance.rate_limits: Self-hosted: unlimited; Cloud API: standard rate limits not documented

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: Local API documentation is well-maintained for data queries. Error responses are JSON. The metrics data model (dimensions, charts, families) requires learning but is consistent.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: All data queries are read-only and fully idempotent.
known_agent_gotchas: Local API has no authentication by default — exposed on :19999; configure for secure deployments
Metrics exist only for the observation window (hours on free tier); queries for old data return nothing
Netdata metric naming follows internal chart.dimension convention — requires familiarization
Cloud API is distinct from local agent API with different endpoints and authentication

security_score_components.security_notes: Local API exposes sensitive system metrics without authentication by default — must configure for production. TLS support available but not default. Cloud API is more secure with JWT. AGPL source is auditable.
